notes: Approved sources for downloading media assets during concept generation. Assets are OPTIONAL — use only when they genuinely enhance the design. Prefer transparent PNGs and lightweight SVGs. Store all downloaded assets in the pass's assets/ subfolder.

policy.downloadPrompt: Only download assets when they would genuinely enhance the design beyond what CSS alone can achieve. A well-crafted CSS gradient or shape is always preferable to a generic stock image. Assets should feel curated and intentional, not decorative filler.

illustrations.name: unDraw
illustrations.prompt: Open-source SVG illustrations with customizable accent colors. Download the SVG and change the fill color to match your palette. Best for empty states, onboarding sections, and hero illustrations. Avoid overuse — one per view maximum.

illustrations.name: Storyset by Freepik
illustrations.prompt: Animated SVG illustrations in multiple styles (Rafiki, Bro, Amico, Pana, Cuate). Each style has a distinct aesthetic. Download as animated SVG for built-in motion. Great for dashboard empty states and loading views.

icons.name: Heroicons
icons.prompt: Clean, minimal icon set. Copy SVG markup directly into HTML — no download needed. Use outline variant for nav items and solid for active states. Consistent stroke width creates professional feel.

icons.name: Phosphor Icons
icons.prompt: Flexible icon family with 6 weight variants (thin, light, regular, bold, fill, duotone). The duotone variant is unique — use it for mid-century or retro styles. Weight variety lets you match the style's typographic density.

icons.name: Lucide Icons
icons.prompt: Community fork of Feather icons with a much larger set. Clean, consistent stroke width. Copy SVG inline for best performance. Good all-purpose icon set.

photos.name: Unsplash
photos.prompt: Use ONLY for specific contextual images that CSS cannot replicate — team avatars, project thumbnails, or hero photography. Download and store locally (do not hotlink). Optimize to webp format if possible. Avoid generic stock photo vibes.

video.name: Coverr
video.prompt: Short video loops for ambient hero backgrounds. Use VERY sparingly — at most one video per entire pass, and only in the hero/dashboard section. Must be muted, autoplay, loop. Abstract/nature textures work best. Keep file size under 2MB.

video.name: Pexels Videos
video.prompt: Alternative video source. Same rules as Coverr — one video max per pass, hero section only, muted autoplay loop. Download smallest resolution that looks good at viewport scale.

lottie-animations.name: LottieFiles
lottie-animations.prompt: Download Lottie JSON animations for loading states, success/error feedback, decorative motion, and micro-interactions. Keep each file under 50kb. Use lottie-web library to play them. Best for polished micro-interactions that would be complex to code from scratch.

textures-patterns.name: Transparent Textures
textures-patterns.prompt: Tileable PNG textures for subtle background patterns. Noise, paper, fabric, concrete textures. Apply at low opacity (5-15%) as CSS background-image over solid colors. Adds tactile depth without overwhelming the design. Brutalist loves concrete textures; mid-century loves paper/linen.

textures-patterns.name: Hero Patterns
textures-patterns.prompt: SVG background patterns encoded as CSS data URIs. No download needed — copy the CSS directly. Customizable foreground and background colors. Use for section backgrounds, card textures, or nav surfaces. Geometric patterns fit mid-century; circuit patterns fit brutalist.
